hero.title: Real-time play, engineered end to end.
hero.intro: XO Arena is a modular, type-safe Tic-Tac-Toe product built around secure identity, server-authoritative matches, live matchmaking, persistent profiles, and installable mobile behavior.

system.title: A small game with production-shaped systems.
system.description: The board is deliberately simple. The engineering around it handles the harder product concerns: identity, concurrency, authority, persistence, responsive delivery, and clear feedback.

signals.title: Secure identity
signals.text: httpOnly sessions, protected user flows, avatar uploads, and validation at trust boundaries.

signals.title: Live rooms
signals.text: Socket.IO matchmaking and room events keep both players synchronized through every turn.

signals.title: Authoritative play
signals.text: A shared, type-safe game engine supports server-validated moves and dependable match state.

signals.title: Adaptive opponents
signals.text: Four computer difficulty modes turn the same game model into progressively stronger challenges.

signals.title: Installable client
signals.text: Responsive, touch-friendly PWA behavior keeps the complete product usable on mobile surfaces.

signals.title: Persistent history
signals.text: Prisma and MySQL retain profiles, match outcomes, move counts, and recent activity.

gallery.title: The complete flow, captured in context.
gallery.description: Each image is served as a compact WebP and loaded only as it approaches the viewport. The frames document the real local product rather than a reconstructed mockup.

items.alt: XO Arena landing page with product introduction, a live board preview, and feature cards
items.title: Product entry
items.description: A theme-aware landing experience explains the game and establishes auth, multiplayer, PWA, and design-system signals before entry.

items.alt: XO Arena lobby showing four computer difficulty levels, quick online matchmaking, and room creation
items.title: Matchmaking lobby
items.description: Players can choose four computer difficulties, enter quick matchmaking, or create an online room from one responsive surface.

items.alt: XO Arena completed match with a Tic-Tac-Toe board, players, match actions, and a seven-turn move log
items.title: Authoritative game room
items.description: The match view combines board state, player identity, replay actions, outcome messaging, and an auditable turn-by-turn move log.

items.alt: XO Arena profile showing nickname and avatar controls alongside a persistent recent-game history table
items.title: Persistent player history
items.description: Profile editing and recent match history make identity and persistence visible, including mode, opponent, outcome, and move count.

deployment.title: Why this is a case study—not a temporary demo.
deployment.paragraph1: XO Arena’s complete experience depends on persistent services: secure sessions, an application server, Socket.IO rooms, a relational database, and uploaded-media storage. Rather than publish a partially provisioned build that misrepresents the product, this page documents the complete local system through its actual recorded flows and product captures.
deployment.paragraph2: The implementation is shaped for deployment; the presentation is intentionally honest about the infrastructure a reliable real-time product requires.

next.title: Explore the systems behind the surfaces.

video.ariaLabel: XO Arena product walkthrough showing authentication, the multiplayer lobby, game rooms, computer opponents, and profile history
